act: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Achterhooks.
activity: noun. The state or quality of being active; activeness.
ait: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Arikem.
att: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Pamplona Atta.
attic: noun. The space, often unfinished and with sloped walls, directly below the roof in the uppermost part of a house or other building,
cat: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Catalan.
catty: adjective. Resembling or characteristic of a cat.
cavity: noun. A hole or hollow depression in a solid object.
cavy: noun. Synonym of domestic guinea pig.
cay: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Cayuga.
city: noun. A large settlement, bigger than a town; sometimes with a specific legal definition, depending on the place.
icy: adjective. Pertaining to, resembling, or abounding in ice; cold; frosty.
ivy: noun. Any of several woody, climbing, or trailing evergreen plants of the genus Hedera.
tacit: adjective. Implied, but not made explicit, especially through silence.
tact: noun. Sensitive mental touch; special skill or faculty; keen perception or discernment; ready power of appreciating and doing what is
tat: symbol. ISO 639-2 & ISO 639-3 language code for Tatar.
tav: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tatuyo.
tic: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tira.
tit: symbol. ISO 639-3 language code for Tinigua.
titi: noun. Any New World monkey of the subfamily Callicebinae, native to South America, distinguished by their long soft fur.
vac: noun. Clipping of vacation (“extended period of time away from work or school”).
vat: noun. A large tub, such as is used for making wine or for tanning.
vatic: adjective. Pertaining to a prophet; prophetic, oracular.
via: noun. A main road or highway, especially in ancient Rome. (Mainly used in set phrases, below.)
viatic: adjective. Of or pertaining to traveling or a journey.
vita: noun. A hagiography; a biography of a saint.
vitta: noun. A fillet, or garland for the head.
